overview.title_en: BK Lab focuses on the synthesis, characterization, and device integration of complex oxide thin films, thermoelectric materials, and ferroelectric heterostructures.
overview.title_kr: 복합 산화물 박막, 열전소재, 강유전 이종구조를 성장·분석하고, 이를 차세대 전자소자와 에너지 소재로 연결하는 연구실
overview.lead: We bridge atomic-scale materials design with device-level functionality — across epitaxial growth, microstructure engineering, and electrical characterization.

topics.title: Multifunctional Oxide Thin Films
topics.summary: Atomic-scale design of complex oxide thin films and heterostructures for emergent electronic and interfacial functionalities.
topics.description: Epitaxial growth and characterization of perovskite, ferroelectric, and multiferroic oxide heterostructures on silicon. We engineer domain structures, interfacial 2D electron gases, and strain states to enable non-volatile memory, sensing, and oxide electronics.

topics.title: Thermoelectric Materials
topics.summary: Microstructure and interface engineering of Bi2Te3-based thermoelectrics to push ZT beyond classical limits.
topics.description: We develop Bi2Te3-based thermoelectrics with tailored microstructures through mechanical deformation, texturing, and atomic-layer interface control. The goal: efficient thermal-to-electrical conversion driven by enhanced phonon scattering and carrier transport.

topics.title: Ferroelectric & Piezoelectric Devices
topics.summary: Ferroelectric thin films and devices for memory, MEMS, neuromorphic electronics, and polarization-driven functionality.
topics.description: BiFeO3, HfZrO2, and PMN-PT thin-film systems for FeFETs, piezoelectric MEMS, and artificial synapses. From polarization switching dynamics and domain-wall motion to wafer-scale device integration on silicon.

highlights.title: Giant Piezoelectricity on Si for Hyperactive MEMS
highlights.summary: Demonstrated record-high piezoelectric response in PMN-PT thin films integrated onto silicon — opening the path to high-performance MEMS actuators.

highlights.title: Ferroelastic switching for nanoscale magnetoelectric devices
highlights.summary: Discovered a deterministic ferroelastic switching pathway in BiFeO3, enabling reproducible non-volatile magnetoelectric control.

highlights.title: Ultrahigh dielectric permittivity in oxide ceramics by hydrogenation
highlights.summary: Achieved >10^5 effective permittivity in protonated oxide ceramics — a step toward miniaturized high-density capacitors.
